body_txt: Although intellectuals usually wear goatees, their "intelligence" has a broad definition. As workers, they'd be quite academic and cerebral; other times, they might be bookish and inventive. As civilians, they could be cultured and erudite, or they could be rational and resourceful. As politicians and leaders, the goatee can indicate that they possess acute political acumen and a natural silver tongue, which allows them to rally and inspire their followers. The goatee allows the audience to quickly identify and recognize The Smart Guy of the team or The Spock . These characters are highly intelligent and guileful; they use their intelligence to defeat, outsmart, and outwit their physically stronger foes. "Intellect" doesn't just refer to geniuses, scientists, or inventors. In Fantasy and Science Fiction , the choice of a long goatee indicates the character is educated in the arts of magic or trained to use their innate psychic powers. In fantasy stories, mages and wizards must study relentlessly to master the arcane arts . In science fiction, psychics are often depicted as having high IQs and having the required mental focus to control their telepathic abilities. They are characterized as being logical and displaying rational behavior. Goatees are believed to have originated in the Beatnik subculture. The goatee was inspired by soul patches, which were popular among Black jazz musicians. Beatniks were anti-materialistic, hated conformity and consumerism, and expressed themselves through arts and crafts. Beatniks believed themselves to be culturally aware and explored existentialism and spiritualism through their works. Beatniks were stereotyped as writers, poets, and other intellectuals, and thus Beat styles are often the default portrayal of intellectuals to this day. In the 20th and 21st centuries, goatees and circle beards were a visual shorthand for an evil character, as influenced by American History X , Breaking Bad , and Star Trek . Goatees temporarily fell out of fashion because they were too iconic as a look for the Evil Counterpart , the Evil Genius , and the Politically Incorrect Villain , to the point of being a cliché. Despite this, the characters were still charismatic, clever, and eloquent. Goes with Good Hair, Evil Hair and circle beards will overlap with Beard of Evil . Can go with Manly Facial Hair . Long goatees can go with Wizard Beard if the wearer is intelligent and well-educated in magic and mysticism. Sister Trope to Soul Patch of Cool , which is when "Soul patches, goatees, and chin tufts are connected to musicians, metalheads, and beatniks." Compare Einstein Hair , which is when scientists or intelligent characters wear long, messy hair. Compare Labcoat of Science and Medicine , which is when lab coats indicate a career in science or medicine.
